subject: Math
summary:
Differentiation is a fundamental concept in calculus that deals with the measurement of rates of change. It is defined as the ratio of the infinitesimal change in the dependent variable to the infinitesimal change in the independent variable. The derivative of a function at a point is often denoted as f'(x) or (d/dx)f(x). In this topic, we will explore various aspects of differentiation including rules and formulas, applications, and common mistakes to avoid.

Differentiation has numerous applications in various fields such as physics, engineering, economics, and computer science. It is used to model real-world phenomena, optimize functions, and make predictions. Some of the key concepts in differentiation include limits, derivatives, and differential equations.

In this summary, we will cover the basic rules and formulas for differentiation, including the power rule, product rule, quotient rule, and chain rule. We will also discuss common mistakes to avoid and provide tips for solving problems effectively.
